//: Minimal ATTACH-mode devcontainer for macolima. Copy this file to
<your-repo>/.devcontainer/devcontainer.json so VS Code can attach cleanly
1. scripts/profile.sh <profile> up        # starts the compose stack
2. VS Code: Remote Explorer → Attach to Running Container →
Do NOT add runArgs, mounts, image, or network here — compose already
configures those (cap_drop, seccomp, internal network, per-profile binds).
Duplicating them in a devcontainer.json either fights compose (start fails)
or — worse — starts a fresh parallel container with weaker settings and
shared state. The three keys below are the ones VS Code actually needs
at attach time; everything else is handled by docker-compose.yml.
name: Claude Sandbox (macolima, attach)
//remoteEnv.SSH_AUTH_SOCK: Empty out the SSH agent socket env var that VS Code Dev Containers auto-injects on every attach. There is no first-class disable knob for this in the Dev Containers extension; remote.SSH.enableAgentForwarding only governs the Remote-SSH extension. Audit 2026-04-25 confirmed the env was being injected with a fresh UUID each attach despite that host setting. With openssh-client purged from the image the socket is unusable, but emptying the env removes the tripwire signal too.
//updateRemoteUserUID: Critical. Without this VS Code runs `usermod` as root during attach to align UIDs, which spawns a root shell that sometimes orphans — the 'stray UID-0 process' drift the tripwire watches for.
//overrideCommand: Keep compose's `sleep infinity` as PID 1. Overriding would replace it with /bin/sh -c 'while sleep ...' and lose tini signal handling.

vscode.//dev.containers.*: Workspace-scoped belt-and-braces for the host VS Code settings that close attach-time host reach. The host settings (~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/settings.json on macOS) are the actual fix; mirroring them here makes the workspace robust if a fresh host machine has them unset. Audit 2026-04-25 caught the IPC credential helper drifting back in (H1) — gitCredentialHelperConfigLocation is the key that closes it, separate from copyGitConfig.
